These factors raise substantial doubt regarding the Company's ability to continue as a going concern.

RiskFactor.name: Shipping Company Dependency Risk
RiskFactor.description: Relying on a limited number of shipping companies to deliver inventory to us and completed orders

RiskFactor.name: Significant Inventory Risk
RiskFactor.description: Exposed to significant inventory risks due to seasonality, new product launches, rapid changes in product cycles and pricing, defective merchandise, changes in customer demand and consumer spending patterns, changes in consumer tastes, spoilage, and other factors.

RiskFactor.name: Statewide Black-out
RiskFactor.description: Prolonged statewide electrical black-out

RiskFactor.name: Stock Repurchase Program
RiskFactor.description: Stock repurchase program may not be fully consummated, might increase stock price volatility, and potentially diminish cash reserves.

RiskFactor.name: Stock-Based Compensation Expense
RiskFactor.description: Expense associated with stock-based compensation.

RiskFactor.name: Strategic transaction risk
RiskFactor.description: Risk related to strategic transactions such as acquisitions and investments, involving integration challenges, financial impacts, regulatory risks, cybersecurity risks, etc.

RiskFactor.name: Stringent and changing data privacy and security laws
RiskFactor.description: We are subject to stringent and changing data privacy and security laws, rules, regulations, and other obligations.

RiskFactor.name: Supervised by ECB
RiskFactor.description: PayPal (Europe) could be deemed a significant supervised entity, and certain activities would become directly supervised by the ECB, increasing compliance costs.

RiskFactor.name: Supplier Commitments
RiskFactor.description: Commitments based on forecasted customer demand that may incur additional costs if reduced

RiskFactor.name: Supplier Disruption
RiskFactor.description: supplier and additional expense and/or production delays as a result of qualifying a new foundry or subcontractor and commencing volume production or testing in the event of a loss of or a decision to add or change a supplier

RiskFactor.name: Supplier Quality Issues
RiskFactor.description: suppliers or their suppliers failing to supply high quality products and/or making changes to their products without qualification

RiskFactor.name: Supplier Risk
RiskFactor.description: Significant suppliers are crucial, and decisions to limit or stop providing merchandise, content, components, or services may impact operations. Supplier violations of laws, regulations, and ethical practices could harm reputation and growth.

RiskFactor.name: Supply Chain Attacks
RiskFactor.description: Cyberattacks introducing malware through software updates affecting software providers including us.

RiskFactor.name: Supply Chain Disruption
RiskFactor.description: Disruption in supply chain affects hardware sales volume and increases costs.

RiskFactor.name: Supply Constraints
RiskFactor.description: Supply constraints due to geopolitical tensions, natural disasters, pandemics, etc.

RiskFactor.name: Supply Shortages
RiskFactor.description: Significant risks of supply shortages and price increases that can materially adversely affect the Company's business, results of operations and financial condition.

RiskFactor.name: Supply chain risks
RiskFactor.description: Lack of guaranteed supply of wafer, component and capacity or decommitment, potential higher wafer and component prices.

RiskFactor.name: Supply-chain Vulnerabilities
RiskFactor.description: Companies rely on products and services from third-party suppliers, exposing them to supply-chain attacks or other business disruptions.

RiskFactor.name: System Damage Risk
RiskFactor.description: Systems and operations could be damaged or interrupted due to events like natural disasters, extreme weather, geopolitical events, security issues, computer viruses, operational failures, causing delay and loss, make product offerings less attractive, and cause liability.

RiskFactor.name: System Interruption Risk
RiskFactor.description: Occasional system interruptions and delays make websites and services slow to respond; could prevent efficient order fulfillment and service provision to customers and third parties, reducing net sales and attractiveness of products and services.

RiskFactor.name: Takeover Defense
RiskFactor.description: Provisions in charter documents and under Delaware law could discourage a takeover considered favorable by stockholders.

RiskFactor.name: Tax Controversy Risk
RiskFactor.description: Risks arising from tax controversies in various jurisdictions affecting operating results and cash flows.

RiskFactor.name: Tax Cuts and Jobs Act of 2017
RiskFactor.description: Global Intangible Low Taxed Income (GILTI) or one-time transition tax.

RiskFactor.name: Tax Impact
RiskFactor.description: U.S. tax rules provide for enhanced accelerated depreciation deductions and the impact of capitalized research and development expenses.

RiskFactor.name: Tax Investigations
RiskFactor.description: We are currently undergoing a number of investigations, audits, and reviews by tax authorities in multiple U.S. and foreign tax jurisdictions. Any adverse outcome of any such audit or review could result in unforeseen tax-related liabilities.

RiskFactor.name: Tax Law Changes
RiskFactor.description: Changes in tax laws, exposure to unanticipated additional tax liabilities, or implementation of reporting or record-keeping obligations could have a material adverse effect on our business.

RiskFactor.name: Tax Liabilities and Collection Obligations
RiskFactor.description: Liabilities related to various taxes and tax collection obligations in the U.S. and numerous foreign jurisdictions.

RiskFactor.name: Tax Liability Risk
RiskFactor.description: Subject to income taxes in various jurisdictions with significant judgment required in determining worldwide provision for income taxes. Audits and disputes may lead to higher effective tax rates and materially affect financial statements.

RiskFactor.name: Tax Risk
RiskFactor.description: Changes in tax rates, adoption of new U.S. or international tax legislation, additional tax liabilities

RiskFactor.name: Tax-related risks
RiskFactor.description: Tax-related risks and uncertainties, including the grantor trust election for the Fire Victim Trust

RiskFactor.name: Technological Competition
RiskFactor.description: Competition in the technology sector involving diverse global companies and specialized firms

RiskFactor.name: Telecommunications Regulation
RiskFactor.description: Laws in several jurisdictions increasingly define certain services as regulated telecommunications services, subjecting them to additional obligations.

RiskFactor.name: Third-Party Attacks
RiskFactor.description: Attacks including cyber-attacks, terrorism, or vandalism

RiskFactor.name: Third-Party Construction
RiskFactor.description: Damage to utility facilities from third-party construction

RiskFactor.name: Third-Party Technology Restrictions
RiskFactor.description: Restrictions from third parties that may impact access to technologies

RiskFactor.name: Third-party Custodian Risk
RiskFactor.description: Risk related to inappropriate access to, theft, or destruction of cryptocurrency assets held by custodian, insufficient insurance coverage, and custodian's failure to maintain effective controls.

RiskFactor.name: Third-party Vendor Risks
RiskFactor.description: Risks from third-party vendors failing to establish adequate processes or cease to exist affecting the Utility's systems.

RiskFactor.name: Trade Barriers
RiskFactor.description: Government actions including trade protection and national security policies could impose trade barriers affecting business.

RiskFactor.name: Trade Regulations
RiskFactor.description: Increasing trade laws, policies, sanctions, and other regulatory requirements affect operations relating to trade and investment.

RiskFactor.name: Trade Restrictions
RiskFactor.description: Inability to sell to a customer due to U.S. or other countries' trade restrictions.

RiskFactor.name: Transaction and Credit Losses
RiskFactor.description: Expense associated with customer protection programs, fraud, chargebacks, and credit losses associated with loans receivable balances.

RiskFactor.name: Uncertain Local Laws
RiskFactor.description: Uncertainty whether Singapore-based service is subject to Singapore law or other local laws requiring a payment processor license.

RiskFactor.name: Uncertain Tax Positions
RiskFactor.description: Recognition and measurement of uncertain tax positions in accordance with U.S. GAAP

RiskFactor.name: Uncertainty of Future Cash Needs
RiskFactor.description: Projections of future cash needs and cash flows are subject to substantial uncertainty.

RiskFactor.name: Undergrounding plan
RiskFactor.description: Review of a 10-year undergrounding plan

RiskFactor.name: Undiscovered Vulnerabilities
RiskFactor.description: undiscovered vulnerabilities in products or services could result in loss of data or intangible property, expose end customers to malicious software programs

RiskFactor.name: Valuation of Goodwill and Intangibles
RiskFactor.description: Requires significant estimates and assumptions in the acquisition method of accounting for business combinations.

RiskFactor.name: Variability in Retail Business
RiskFactor.description: Demand for products and services can fluctuate significantly due to seasonality, promotions, product launches, global economic conditions such as recessionary fears or rising inflation, disasters, extreme weather, or geopolitical events.

RiskFactor.name: Variability in Revenue
RiskFactor.description: Variability in revenue and income from entering into, amending, or terminating agreements or relationships.

RiskFactor.name: Weather Conditions
RiskFactor.description: California has experienced unprecedented weather conditions in recent years.

RiskFactor.name: Weather Events
RiskFactor.description: Increasing frequency and intensity related to climate change.

RiskFactor.name: Web Spam
RiskFactor.description: Web spam and content farms could decrease search quality and damage reputation.

RiskFactor.name: Wildfire Fund Contribution
RiskFactor.description: Financial contribution to wildfire fund by IOUs borne by shareholders

RiskFactor.name: Wildfire Fund risks
RiskFactor.description: Risks related to the Wildfire Fund and other provisions of AB 1054 not effectively mitigating the risk of liability for damages arising from catastrophic wildfires

RiskFactor.name: Wildfire Insurance Settlement
RiskFactor.description: Settlement dated October 7, 2022 affects revenue requirement request

RiskFactor.name: Wildfire Investigations
RiskFactor.description: The Utility is a target of a number of investigations, including those related to wildfires.

RiskFactor.name: Wildfire Liability Insurance
RiskFactor.description: Insurance based on self-insurance funded through CPUC-jurisdictional rates for test year 2023 and future years.

RiskFactor.name: Wildfire Mitigation and Catastrophic Events Cost Recovery Applications
RiskFactor.description: Applications related to cost recovery for wildfire mitigation and certain catastrophic events.

RiskFactor.name: Wildfire ignition risk
RiskFactor.description: Risk associated with wildfires

RiskFactor.name: Wildfire liabilities
RiskFactor.description: Liabilities related to wildfires, including claims, fire suppression costs, penalties, and fines.

RiskFactor.name: Wildfire-Related Liabilities
RiskFactor.description: Potential liabilities related to wildfires including settlements, causes, damages, and personal injuries.

RiskFactor.name: Workforce Aging
RiskFactor.description: The Utility's workforce is aging, and many employees are eligible to retire, potentially leading to a shortage of experienced personnel.

RiskFactor.name: Workplace Hazards
RiskFactor.description: Failure to identify and control workplace hazards

RiskFactor.name: Zero Day Vulnerabilities
RiskFactor.description: Exploiting unknown vulnerabilities in products such as Exchange Server on-premises.

RiskFactor.name: additional risks
RiskFactor.description: Our credit products expose us to additional risks

RiskFactor.name: bad advertising practices
RiskFactor.description: Stopping bad advertising practices and protecting users on the web

RiskFactor.name: catastrophic events
RiskFactor.description: items resulting from catastrophic events

RiskFactor.name: competition in AI offerings
RiskFactor.description: Our AI offerings compete with AI products from hyperscalers such as Amazon Bedrock, Amazon CodeWhisperer, and Google AI, as well as products from other emerging competitors, including Meta's LLaMA2 and other open source solutions.

RiskFactor.name: competition in cloud services
RiskFactor.description: Azure faces diverse competition from companies such as Amazon, Google, IBM, Oracle, VMware, and open source offerings.

RiskFactor.name: competition in security solutions
RiskFactor.description: Our Enterprise Mobility + Security offerings also compete with identity vendors, security solution vendors, and numerous other security point solution vendors.

RiskFactor.name: competition in server products
RiskFactor.description: Our server products face competition from a wide variety of server operating systems and applications offered by companies with a range of market approaches.

RiskFactor.name: cross-border trade issues
RiskFactor.description: Factors reducing cross-border trade or making such trade more difficult could harm our business.

RiskFactor.name: currency volatilities
RiskFactor.description: Currency volatilities may continue, which may significantly impact our reported results and consolidated trends and comparisons.

RiskFactor.name: fire risk mitigation
RiskFactor.description: capital expenditures excluded from Utility's equity rate base pursuant to AB 1054

RiskFactor.name: foreign currency exchange risk
RiskFactor.description: The risk related to the fluctuation in foreign currency exchange rates affecting earnings, assets, and liabilities.

RiskFactor.name: fraud and abusive behaviors
RiskFactor.description: Failure to deal effectively with fraud, abusive behaviors, bad transactions, and negative customer experiences may increase our loss rate and could negatively impact our business.

RiskFactor.name: inflation in health care costs
RiskFactor.description: Continued high inflation in health care costs

RiskFactor.name: market risk
RiskFactor.description: Quantitative and qualitative disclosures about market risk

RiskFactor.name: personal data transfer restrictions
RiskFactor.description: Restrictions related to the transfer of personal data to countries outside the EEA and similar data privacy laws in other jurisdictions, including China.

RiskFactor.name: third-party provider risks
RiskFactor.description: Risks inherent in engaging and relying upon third-party providers include legal, regulatory, information security, reputational and operational risks.
